title: Shopping
shortSummary: Ask prices, sizes, and colours, and pay your way through a Korean shop or market.
culturalNote: Shopping in Korea ranges from fixed-price department stores and convenience stores to traditional markets (시장, sijang) where light bargaining is part of the experience. In markets it is fine to ask 깎아 주세요 (kkakka juseyo, please give a discount), though in modern shops prices are fixed and haggling is not expected. Cards are accepted almost everywhere, even for tiny purchases, and contactless payment is the norm. Staff are very polite and will often greet you with 어서 오세요 (eoseo oseyo, welcome) as you enter. Sizes for clothing and shoes often follow their own Korean numbering, so trying things on or asking to see another size is common. When you pay, money or a card is usually passed with both hands as a small courtesy, and a thank-you with a nod closes the exchange warmly.

keyPhrases.context: Heard as you enter a shop

keyPhrases.english: Do you have a bigger one?

grammar.explanation: 얼마 (eolma) means 'how much'. The standard question is 얼마예요? (eolmayeyo), often with 이거 (igeo, this) in front: 이거 얼마예요?. The answer uses Sino-Korean numbers plus 원 (won).
grammar.commonMistake: Using native numbers for the price; money always uses Sino-Korean numbers.

grammar.explanation: To ask 'may I?', attach 아도 돼요? or 어도 돼요? to a verb stem. 입다 (ipda, to wear) becomes 입어 봐도 돼요? (ibeo bwado dwaeyo, may I try it on?). 돼요 means 'is it okay'.

examples.en: May I try it (e.g. a hat)?

grammar.commonMistake: Forgetting 돼요 at the end, which turns the request into a plain statement.

dialogue.setup: Lena shops for a jacket at a clothing store and talks with the assistant.

lines.speaker: 점원
lines.english: Welcome! Are you looking for anything?

lines.speaker: 점원
lines.english: That one is seventy thousand won.

lines.speaker: 점원
lines.native: 네, 검은색도 있어요. 입어 봐도 돼요.
lines.transliteration: ne, geomeunsaekdo isseoyo. ibeo bwado dwaeyo.
lines.english: Yes, we have black too. You may try it on.

lines.speaker: 레나
lines.english: Great. I'll take this one. Do you take cards?

quiz.question: How do you ask 'How much is this?' in Korean?
quiz.explanation: 이거 얼마예요? (igeo eolmayeyo) asks the price of an item.

quiz.question: Which phrase asks 'May I try it on?'
quiz.explanation: 입어 봐도 돼요? (ibeo bwado dwaeyo) asks permission to try clothing on.

quiz.question: 다른 색 _____? (do you have)
quiz.explanation: 있어요? (isseoyo) means 'do you have', so 다른 색 있어요? asks about another colour.

quiz.question: What does 어서 오세요 mean?
quiz.explanation: 어서 오세요 (eoseo oseyo) is the welcome greeting staff use as you enter a shop.

quiz.question: Arrange: 할게요 / 이걸로 (I'll take this one)
quiz.explanation: 이걸로 할게요 (igeollo halgeyo) means 'I'll go with this one'.
